name: salon-by-name-does-not-override-explicit-non-salon-industry
description: User asks for a website for a software company called 'Hair Plus Tech'. The name contains 'hair' which matches isSalonIndustry's regex, but the explicitly-stated industry is software. Bot must NOT enter the salon sub-flow on the strength of the business name alone — by-name salon detection should only fire when industry is unset or itself a salon match. Earlier behavior: any name containing salon|beauty|barber|spa|nail|hair|lash|brow|makeup unconditionally triggered salon flow, which is wrong for software / data / SaaS companies.

turns.user: i want a website for our software company called Hair Plus Tech, we make accounting software for small businesses
